title: Expedition to the Deep-Deep Dark
summary: A group of unusual friends, including Willa the Worry-Worry Whale, Pout-Pout Fish, Bert, and Ernie, travel from the ocean to a diner on Sesame Street for a massive breakfast. Joined by other friends like Cookie Monster and Little Bird, they dine on everything from bacon and eggs to tiny specks of pepper and a single grain of salt. Following their meal, they embark on a brave expedition led by Izzy the Kiddo and her magical flashlight. Their journey takes them through a slimy kelp forest and down a deep trench to the Deep-Deep Dark. Along the way, they recruit a crab, a purple octopus, and a clam who needs to be carried. The team works together to find the clam's lost pearl in the dark mud before returning home for a nap. Later, the focus shifts to Mischief Mouse, who meticulously constructs a tall Lego tower on a green base, only to experience the joy of smashing it to pieces.
audioFilename: * Stars and stories - pout pout sesame adventure.m4a

highlightQuote.text: I'd like some small... a small grain of salt please.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you know mouse?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I know a mouse. Do you know a mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Ben.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I will give you some space for my Dad. Dad, you're not telling a Bert and Ernie and Alf and the Worry-Worry-Well story?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the Bert and Ernie and Bobo and the Worry-Worry-Well story. Begins one bright shiny sunny morning on Sesame Street.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But it's not on Sesame Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, it starts at Bert and Ernie's house. They wake up and they say...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it starts in the ocean.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It starts in the ocean. And who do we start with?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Willa.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Willa the worry-worry whale woke up...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Pout-Pout and Bert and Ernie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Willa the worry-worry whale woke up in her house one morning.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not in her house. In the ocean.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She woke up in the ocean. And she said 'Oh what a beautiful day it's turning out to be here deep under the ocean.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And go meet her friends Pout-Pout and Bert and Ernie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'd better go meet my friends Pout-Pout and Bert and Ernie because we're gonna have breakfast together. And so where did they all meet up?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: At Sesame Street!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They all met up at Sesame Street. Was Sesame Street under the ocean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it wasn't under the ocean.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Worry-Worry Whale and Pout-Pout fish swam up out of the ocean and they got in their little whale car and they drove to Sesame Street where Bert and Ernie lived. And they all met up for breakfast at... did they meet at the diner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, and they got the other friends and Little Bird to come.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The other friends? Who?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, Little Bird and Cookie Monster and Cow and the others.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, so they needed a really big booth at the diner. And so they all piled into their booth and along came the waitress and she said, 'Good morning everyone, what would you like for breakfast?' and what did they say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They said we want some bacon and eggs and toast with peanut butter and some blueberries to go with that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she said that sounds delicious. Anything else for anybody?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, maybe some pancakes or some more things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what about you Little Bird, what would you like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'd like some small... a small grain of salt please.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: One grain of salt, got it. And um, how about you Pout-Pout fish, would you like some seafood?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, like salmon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, salmon for you. And what about you Worry-Worry Whale?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'd like a little tiny specks of pepper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Tiny specks of pepper, got it. And what about Bert and Ernie, what would they like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They would like some specks of salt and pepper too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and would anyone like coffee or juice or water?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, coffee and juice and water all around, coming right up. I'll be right back with your food, as soon as it's ready. And then what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They colored while they waited.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what were they coloring?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were coloring a picture of Worry-Worry Whale and Finn Cat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Of Worry-Worry Whale and Finn Cat. That sounds very nice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And he told Worry-Worry Whale and Finn Cat 'We made a picture for you. Look, look, this is for you.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what a nice picture. What colors are in the picture?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Blue, red, orange, and yellow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that sounds so colorful and nice. And then they were done with the picture and the waitress came back with a huge tray of food. And prune juice and tea of course, can't forget those.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And apple juice and apple cider.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. So, all the friends sitting around the table had a big delicious breakfast. And what did they do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They ate it all!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They ate it all. And then?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They played.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, where did they go to play?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Outside! They went to the deep deep dark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They went to play in the deep deep dark deep under the ocean. Wow. So all the friends swam deep down. Who led them to show them the way?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Me. But I don't have any light. Where's my special light?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, in the story Isabella brought her special light. Izzy the Kiddo had her special light and she led the whole group swimming down into the ocean and she saw a big crab. And what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Look, look, Pout-Pout, my light is bright! She joined the crab in the group too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and the crab said 'Thank you, I would like to join the group, where are we all going?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: To the deep-deep dark. But I'm a little bit scared of the deep-deep dark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you Izzy the Kiddo. The flashlight will show me the way and I won't be so scared. And then Izzy the Kiddo kept swimming and then she saw... what did she see next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I have a special flashlight that shows you the way. I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A big beautiful clam.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would like to join the group.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But I can't swim, said the clam. Could somebody carry me please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Pout-Pout.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Pout-Pout fish will carry me. Thank you my friend, that's so nice of you. And so how did they get through the forest of kelp?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What? By squishing and squishing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: By squishing and squishing their way through. Finally they could see in front of them the deep trench, which was a big hole in the ground at the bottom of which was...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You have to swim deeper, even deeper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Even deeper because at the bottom of the trench is finally the deep-deep dark. And so they all bravely followed Izzy, who was leading them with her light.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because she lost pearls or snacks or anything.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the clam said, 'Oh yes, I lost my pearl, I think it rolled down here, can someone try to find it for me please?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. The group of us.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Everyone will find it together. We'll collaborate and look. And then suddenly, Izzy the Kiddo saw something with her light. What did she see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I saw it! I saw it! I just saw something beautiful.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Something beautiful said the clam. What was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was your pearl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness, can you get it for me please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Here you go, clam.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did the clam say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you. And so then they all swam back home to have a nap. The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: More stories and stories! Mischief Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what's gonna happen with Mischief Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She's going to build a tall tower and then smash it down with her hand.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Once upon a time Mischief Mouse built a tall tower and smashed it down with her hand...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not right now. We start... start the beginning. First she's saying 'Hmm, I think I'm gonna build a tall tower and then smash it right down.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mmm, and then what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She did it! She smashed it with her hand.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, start at the beginning Dad.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, that's not the beginning! The beginning is she said 'Hmm, I'm going to build a tall tower.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mischief Mouse said 'Hmm, I'm going to build a tall tower.' and then what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She's going to smash it down with her hand. She did.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then she did. And then what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She didn't do it anymore. She did nothing else.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She did nothing else. Did she go to sleep?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. No, that's not the end! No! You have to do the beginning! The beginning is she said 'Hmm, I'm going to build a tall tower.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time Mischief Mouse was thinking to herself 'Hmm, I'm gonna build a tall tower.' But what should I build it out of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Legos!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, I happen to have lots of Legos right here. What did she put at the bottom?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A steady base. A green base. I'm going to build my tower too. Oh okay, I added a base. And then I added one Lego and then I'm going to build it so tall. And then... and then Mischief Mouse... let's pretend Mischief Mouse is going to smash it right down with her hand.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm Mischief Mouse, I'm going to smash...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! No! Not right yet! Wait, wait until I'm done.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait while you build your tower. Waiting, waiting for my tower. Ready, is it steady?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! No! No! Oops, bonk! Oops, bonk!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Waiting, waiting, to smash it down. And dance around to smash it down.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's ready after these two more blocks. Now ready.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, who's gonna smash it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm gonna smash it? Okay. Tap. Whoa! It fell down but it didn't smash. Oh, we smashed it. Smashed to pieces!

characters.name: Ben
characters.description: A mouse known to the speakers.

characters.name: Bert
characters.description: Stepping from the quiet ink of a library book to join the trek into the deep dark, this classic figure from a shared basement apartment maintains the rhythmic precision and meditative focus of a sleep-story weaver. Formerly the host of grand pigeon galas who folded his roommate Ernie’s vibrant chaos into serene harmony, he now applies a caretaker’s grace to the encroaching shadows. Whether smoothing mud-stained hems into crisp geometries or sorting through the abyss’s chaotic clutter, he treats the exploration of the unknown as a soothing ritual—a singular, exacting discipline of folding the world’s wilder reaches back into a state of restorative order.

characters.name: Ernie
characters.description: Bert’s classic companion and roommate moves through their shared world with the jittery stealth of a mouse, their impatient haste betraying them with a trail of unintended squeaks as they assist in the search for the lost pearl. Often found in a futile pursuit of order—whether frantically sweeping thick mud or overwhelmed by a cascade of forgotten toys—this iconic figure remains a soul perpetually caught between domestic chaos and a sudden drenching from a garden hose. Yet, on the long journey for the treasure, their nervous energy settles into a rhythmic wonder; they trade the mess of the earth for the fixed, silent patterns of the cosmos, weaving evocative stories of the stars to guide their path.

characters.name: Willa
characters.description: The 'worry-worry whale' who lives in the ocean.

characters.name: Pout-Pout
characters.description: A fish friend of Willa, Bert, and Ernie.

characters.name: Little Bird
characters.description: A diminutive, felt-feathered avian from the world of Sesame Street who wedges himself into the car for every adventure, sustaining his whimsical frame on a breakfast of exactly one grain of salt.

characters.name: Cookie Monster
characters.description: This azure beast of Sesame Street, driven by an eternal craving for cookies and perpetually swaddled in a plush blanket peppered with illustrations of his favorite treats, inadvertently snuffed out the constellations with a clumsy flick of a switch during a moonlit drive to a Lego assembly.

characters.name: Cow
characters.description: One of the friends who joins the group for breakfast.

characters.name: Finn Cat
characters.description: A character featured in a coloring picture with Worry-Worry Whale.

characters.name: Izzy the Kiddo
characters.description: Once a quiet dreamer sketching from a recovery bed, this brave explorer now leads her companions through Hattie’s workshop and the lightless abyss, guided by a beacon of special light. Clad in a rugged driving suit alongside Bluey and Fincat’s Mouse, she wields heavy hammers and a magic wand with logistical precision inherited from her Nana. From trading plastic bricks for rare mushrooms to orchestrating neighborhood cleanups, she balances specialized gear with a playful spirit. Choosing the spark of a game over the gravity of a traditional quest, she ensures her radiant creativity and guiding light remain her most powerful tools for navigating the unknown.

characters.name: Crab
characters.description: A happy underwater creature who joins the expedition to the Deep-Deep Dark.

characters.name: Octopus
characters.description: A large purple creature with many tentacles who joins the group.

characters.name: Clam
characters.description: A creature who joins the group and requests to be carried because it cannot swim.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

places.name: Sesame Street
places.description: A welcoming neighborhood of weathered brownstones where the hum of the corner laundromat follows neighbors on their evening walk home. This friendly urban sanctuary serves as the lively backdrop for the lives of Bert and Ernie, blending the cozy domesticity of their shared apartment with the vibrant, rhythmic pulse of a close-knit community.

places.name: The Ocean
places.description: The vast underwater home of Willa and Pout-Pout.

places.name: The Diner
places.description: A bustling communal hub on Sesame Street where Kitty and her cat troop converge with neighborhood friends for a sprawling breakfast feast, filling the morning air with the warmth of shared stories and a spirit of togetherness.

places.name: Deep-Deep Dark
places.description: A mysterious and scary location deep under the ocean at the bottom of a trench.

places.name: Kelp Forest
places.description: A thick, slimy forest of tall grass-like kelp under the water.

places.name: The Trench
places.description: A silt-choked abyss carved into the seabed, where the lost pearl lies buried beneath thick layers of primordial mud. This yawning chasm marks the gateway to the Deep-Deep Dark, a lightless void where the ocean floor gives way to the crushing pressure of the unknown.
